title: Book Doctor Appointment
steps: Step 1: Open your phone and tap the browser app (Chrome or Safari).
Step 2: Type 'Practo' or 'Apollo' in the search bar and press Search.
Step 3: Tap 'Find Doctors' and type your doctor's name or health problem.
Step 4: Choose a doctor from the list and tap 'Book Appointment'.
Step 5: Select a date and time that works for you.
Step 6: Enter your name and phone number when asked.
Step 7: Tap 'Confirm Booking'. You will get a message on your phone.
warning: If you feel very unwell, please call 108 immediately instead of booking online.

title: Order Medicines Online
steps: Step 1: Open your phone and tap the browser or PharmEasy / Netmeds app.
Step 2: Tap the search bar and type the name of your medicine.
Step 3: Find your medicine in the list and tap on it.
Step 4: Tap 'Add to Cart' and then tap 'Go to Cart'.
Step 5: Upload a photo of your doctor's prescription if asked.
Step 6: Enter your home address for delivery.
Step 7: Choose 'Cash on Delivery' if you do not want to pay online.
Step 8: Tap 'Place Order'. Your medicine will arrive in 1-3 days.
warning: Always order medicines only with a valid doctor's prescription. Do not self-medicate.

title: Find Nearby Hospital
steps: Step 1: Open Google Maps on your phone (blue and white map icon).
Step 2: Tap the search bar at the top of the screen.
Step 3: Type 'hospital near me' and tap the Search button.
Step 4: A list of nearby hospitals will appear on the screen.
Step 5: Tap on any hospital name to see its address and phone number.
Step 6: Tap 'Directions' to get step-by-step directions from your location.
Step 7: You can also tap 'Call' to directly call the hospital.
warning: In a medical emergency, call 108 immediately. Do not drive yourself to the hospital.

title: Call Ambulance
steps: Step 1: Stay calm and do not panic.
Step 2: Pick up your phone and open the dialer (green phone icon).
Step 3: Dial 1 - 0 - 8 and press the green call button.
Step 4: Tell the person your name and address clearly.
Step 5: Describe the emergency — for example, 'chest pain' or 'person fell down'.
Step 6: Stay on the line and follow their instructions.
Step 7: Unlock your front door so the ambulance team can enter easily.
warning: 108 is free to call at any time, day or night. Do not hesitate to call in an emergency.

title: Set Medicine Reminder
steps: Step 1: Open the Clock app on your phone (it looks like a clock face).
Step 2: Tap 'Alarm' at the bottom of the screen.
Step 3: Tap the '+' button to add a new alarm.
Step 4: Set the time you need to take your medicine.
Step 5: Tap 'Label' or 'Name' and type the medicine name.
Step 6: Choose the days you need the reminder (e.g., every day).
Step 7: Tap 'Save'. Your phone will ring at that time every day.
warning: Do not skip your medicines even if you feel better. Always complete the full course.

title: Download Vaccination Certificate
steps: Step 1: Open your phone browser and go to cowin.gov.in.
Step 2: Tap 'Register / Sign In Yourself' on the home page.
Step 3: Enter your mobile number and tap 'Get OTP'.
Step 4: Check your SMS for the OTP number and type it in.
Step 5: Tap on your name from the list of members shown.
Step 6: Tap 'Certificate' to view your vaccination certificate.
Step 7: Tap 'Download' to save it to your phone as a PDF.
warning: Keep your vaccination certificate saved safely. You may need it for travel or hospital visits.

title: Pay Electricity Bill
steps: Step 1: Open Google Pay, PhonePe, or Paytm app on your phone.
Step 2: Tap 'Electricity' or 'Bill Payments' on the home screen.
Step 3: Select your electricity provider (e.g., TNEB for Tamil Nadu).
Step 4: Enter your Consumer Number from your electricity bill.
Step 5: Tap 'Fetch Bill' to see the amount due.
Step 6: Confirm the amount and tap 'Pay Now'.
Step 7: Enter your UPI PIN to complete the payment.
Step 8: Save or take a screenshot of the payment receipt.
warning: Never share your UPI PIN or OTP with anyone, even if they claim to be from the bank or electricity board.

title: Pay Water Bill
steps: Step 1: Open Google Pay, PhonePe, or Paytm app on your phone.
Step 2: Tap 'Water' or 'Bill Payments' on the home screen.
Step 3: Select your city's water board from the list.
Step 4: Enter your Consumer ID or Connection Number from your water bill.
Step 5: Tap 'Fetch Bill' to see your outstanding amount.
Step 6: Verify the amount and tap 'Pay Now'.
Step 7: Enter your UPI PIN to complete the payment.
Step 8: Screenshot the confirmation page as proof of payment.
warning: Always pay bills only through official apps like Google Pay or Paytm. Do not pay through unknown links sent via SMS or WhatsApp.

title: Recharge Mobile
steps: Step 1: Open Google Pay, PhonePe, or Paytm app on your phone.
Step 2: Tap 'Mobile Recharge' on the home screen.
Step 3: Enter the mobile number you want to recharge.
Step 4: Your network provider (Airtel, Jio, BSNL, Vi) will be detected automatically.
Step 5: Choose a recharge plan from the list or enter a custom amount.
Step 6: Tap 'Proceed' and review the details.
Step 7: Enter your UPI PIN to complete the payment.
Step 8: You will receive an SMS confirming the recharge.
warning: Recharge only your own number or numbers of people you know. Beware of fake calls asking you to recharge unknown numbers.

title: Send Money Using UPI
steps: Step 1: Open Google Pay or PhonePe on your phone.
Step 2: Tap 'Send Money' or 'Transfer' on the home screen.
Step 3: Enter the recipient's mobile number or UPI ID.
Step 4: Type the amount you want to send.
Step 5: Add a note if needed (e.g., 'rent' or 'groceries').
Step 6: Tap 'Pay' and carefully review the receiver's name shown on screen.
Step 7: Enter your UPI PIN only after confirming the correct person.
Step 8: Save the transaction ID shown on the success screen.
warning: Always verify the receiver's name before entering your UPI PIN. Never send money to unknown persons. You will NEVER be asked for your PIN to RECEIVE money — that is a scam.

title: Check Bank Balance
steps: Step 1: Open your bank's official app (e.g., SBI YONO, Canara ai1, IOB Mobile).
Step 2: Log in using your MPIN or fingerprint.
Step 3: Tap 'Account Summary' or 'My Accounts' on the home screen.
Step 4: Your current balance will be displayed on the screen.
Step 5: Alternatively, you can give a missed call to your bank's balance enquiry number.
Step 7: Your balance will be sent to your registered mobile number by SMS.
warning: Use only your bank's official app. Do not check your balance on public Wi-Fi or shared phones.

title: Download Bank Statement
steps: Step 1: Open your bank's official mobile app and log in.
Step 2: Tap 'Accounts' or 'Account Summary'.
Step 3: Select the account for which you need a statement.
Step 4: Tap 'Statement' or 'Transaction History'.
Step 5: Choose the date range (e.g., last 3 months or last 6 months).
Step 6: Tap 'Download' or 'View Statement'.
Step 7: The statement will be saved as a PDF on your phone.
Step 8: You can find it in your phone's Downloads folder.
warning: Bank statements contain sensitive personal information. Do not share them with unknown persons or agencies.

title: Block ATM Card
steps: Step 1: Do NOT panic. Act quickly to protect your money.
Step 2: Call your bank's 24-hour helpline immediately.
Step 3: SBI: 1800112211 | Canara Bank: 18004250018 | Indian Bank: 18004250000.
Step 4: Press the option for 'Block ATM Card' in the phone menu.
Step 5: Provide your account number and registered mobile number when asked.
Step 6: Confirm the card block. You will get an SMS confirmation.
Step 7: Alternatively, open your bank app → Cards → Block Card.
Step 8: Visit your nearest branch to request a new replacement card.
warning: Block your card IMMEDIATELY if it is lost or stolen. Do not wait. Even a few minutes delay can result in financial loss.

title: Pay Gas Cylinder Bill
steps: Step 1: Open Google Pay, PhonePe, or Paytm app on your phone.
Step 2: Tap 'LPG Gas' or 'Bill Payments' on the home screen.
Step 3: Select your gas provider — Indane, HP Gas, or Bharat Gas.
Step 4: Enter your LPG Consumer ID or registered mobile number.
Step 5: Tap 'Book Cylinder' or 'Pay Bill' as needed.
Step 6: Confirm the amount and delivery address shown.
Step 7: Enter your UPI PIN to complete the booking and payment.
Step 8: Note the booking reference number sent to your phone.
warning: Book your gas cylinder only through official apps or by calling 7718955555 (Indane). Ignore unknown callers offering free or discounted cylinders — these are scams.

title: Apply for Senior Citizen Pension
steps: Step 1: Visit your nearest Ration Shop or Common Service Centre (CSC) in your village or town.
Step 2: Ask for the 'Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme' (IGNOAPS) application form.
Step 3: Fill in your name, age, address, Aadhaar number, and bank account details.
Step 4: Attach a photocopy of your Aadhaar card, age proof, and bank passbook.
Step 5: Submit the form to the Village Administrative Officer (VAO) or Block Development Office.
Step 6: Collect the acknowledgement receipt with your application reference number.
Step 7: Your application will be verified within 30 days and pension credited to your bank account.
warning: You must be 60 years or older and belong to a Below Poverty Line (BPL) family to be eligible. Do not pay anyone to apply — this service is completely free.

title: Check Pension Status
steps: Step 1: Open your phone browser and go to nsap.nic.in.
Step 2: Tap 'Reports' on the top menu and then tap 'Beneficiary Search'.
Step 3: Select your State as Tamil Nadu from the dropdown.
Step 4: Enter your Aadhaar number or application reference number.
Step 5: Tap 'Search' to view your pension application status.
Step 6: The page will show whether your application is Approved, Pending, or Rejected.
Step 7: If approved, your pension amount and payment dates will also be displayed.
warning: If your pension has not arrived for more than 2 months, visit your nearest Block Development Office with your Aadhaar card and bank passbook to raise a complaint.

title: Download Aadhaar Card
steps: Step 1: Open your phone browser and go to myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in.
Step 2: Tap 'Download Aadhaar' on the home page.
Step 3: Enter your 12-digit Aadhaar number in the box.
Step 4: Enter the Captcha code shown on the screen (the letters and numbers in the image).
Step 5: Tap 'Send OTP' — a one-time password will be sent to your registered mobile number.
Step 6: Enter the OTP and tap 'Verify and Download'.
Step 7: The e-Aadhaar PDF will download to your phone. The PDF password is your PIN code (first 4 digits) + birth year (e.g., 600001990).
warning: Download your Aadhaar only from the official UIDAI website uidai.gov.in. Do not use third-party websites — they may steal your personal information.

title: Update Aadhaar Details
steps: Step 1: Open your phone browser and go to myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in.
Step 2: Tap 'Update Aadhaar Online' on the home page.
Step 3: Log in using your Aadhaar number and OTP sent to your registered mobile.
Step 4: Select what you want to update — Name, Address, Date of Birth, or Mobile Number.
Step 5: Enter the corrected details carefully.
Step 6: Upload a scanned copy or photo of your supporting document (e.g., Voter ID, Passport, Utility Bill).
Step 7: Pay the update fee of Rs. 50 online using UPI or Debit Card.
Step 8: Note the Update Request Number (URN) to track your update status later.
warning: Carry original documents when visiting an Aadhaar Seva Kendra for in-person updates. Do not share your Aadhaar OTP with anyone offering to update your Aadhaar on your behalf — this is a common scam.

title: Apply for Government Scheme
steps: Step 1: Open your phone browser and go to tnschemes.tn.gov.in for Tamil Nadu schemes.
Step 2: Browse the list of available schemes or tap the Search bar to find a specific scheme.
Step 3: Tap on the scheme name to read the eligibility conditions clearly.
Step 4: If you are eligible, tap 'Apply Now' or note the office where you must apply.
Step 5: Fill in the application form with your personal details, Aadhaar number, and bank account.
Step 6: Upload or carry required documents such as Aadhaar, income certificate, and community certificate.
Step 7: Submit the form online or at your nearest Village Administrative Office (VAO).
Step 8: Keep the acknowledgement receipt safely for future reference.
warning: All government schemes are free to apply. If anyone asks you to pay money to process your application, refuse and report them to the nearest police station or call 1930.

title: Book Railway Ticket with Senior Concession
steps: Step 1: Open the IRCTC Rail Connect app or go to irctc.co.in on your browser.
Step 2: Log in with your IRCTC username and password. If you do not have one, register for free.
Step 3: Enter your 'From' station, 'To' station, and date of travel.
Step 4: Tap 'Search Trains' and choose your preferred train from the list.
Step 5: Select your class (Sleeper, AC 3-Tier, etc.) and tap 'Book Now'.
Step 6: Enter passenger details — name, age, and select 'Senior Citizen' under the passenger category.
Step 7: The discounted fare will be applied automatically (40% for men, 50% for women aged 60+).
Step 8: Complete payment using UPI or Debit Card. Save the ticket PDF sent to your email or phone.
warning: Carry your original Aadhaar card or any government ID as age proof during your journey. The ticket checker (TTE) may ask for it to verify your senior citizen concession.

title: Make a WhatsApp Video Call
steps: Step 1: Open the WhatsApp app on your phone (green icon).
Step 2: Tap on the contact name you want to call.
Step 3: Tap the small video camera icon at the top right.
Step 4: Wait for the other person to answer.
Step 5: Speak normally and hold the phone steady.
Step 6: Tap the red button to end the call.
warning: Only accept video calls from people you know. Do not answer unknown international numbers.

title: Set Emergency Contact on Phone
steps: Step 1: Open the Settings app on your phone.
Step 2: Tap 'Safety & Emergency' or search for 'Emergency'.
Step 3: Tap 'Emergency Contacts'.
Step 4: Tap 'Add Contact'.
Step 5: Choose a trusted family member.
Step 6: Save the contact.
Step 7: In emergency, press the power button 5 times quickly to activate SOS.
warning: Choose someone who is always reachable and knows your medical condition.

title: Use Voice Assistant to Make a Call
steps: Step 1: Unlock your phone.
Step 2: Say 'Hey Google' or press and hold the home button.
Step 3: Say 'Call my son' or say the person's name clearly.
Step 4: The phone will automatically dial the number.
Step 5: Speak normally during the call.
Step 6: Tap the red button to end the call.
warning: Make sure the contact name is saved correctly in your phone for voice calling to work properly.

title: Book Safe Taxi for Seniors
steps: Step 1: Open Uber or Ola app.
Step 2: Enter your pickup location.
Step 3: Enter your destination.
Step 4: Choose the cab type.
Step 5: Confirm driver details shown on screen.
Step 6: Share trip details with a family member.
Step 7: Sit in the cab and confirm driver name matches app.
warning: Never enter a vehicle if the driver name or vehicle number does not match the app details.

title: Listen to  Songs on YouTube
steps: Step 1: Open the YouTube app.
Step 2: Tap the search icon.
Step 3: Type the name of the devotional song or god.
Step 4: Tap on the video from trusted channels.
Step 5: Adjust volume if needed.
Step 6: Tap pause when finished.
warning: Avoid clicking unknown advertisements that ask for payment or personal details.

title: Book Gas Cylinder by Phone Call
steps: Step 1: Keep your registered mobile number with you.
Step 2: Dial 7718955555 (Indane automatic booking number).
Step 3: Follow the voice instructions carefully.
Step 4: Confirm your LPG ID when asked.
Step 5: You will receive a booking confirmation SMS.
Step 6: Note the booking number for reference.
warning: Book gas only using official company numbers. Do not trust unknown callers offering discounts.

title: Get Directions Using Google Maps
steps: Step 1: Unlock your phone.
Step 2: Open the Google Maps app (colorful pin icon).
Step 3: Tap the search bar at the top.
Step 4: Type the place name (Example: Apollo Hospital Chennai).
Step 5: Tap the correct place from the list.
Step 6: Tap the 'Directions' button.
Step 7: Choose travel mode (Car, Bus, Walking).
Step 8: Tap 'Start' to begin navigation.
Step 9: Follow the voice instructions carefully.
warning: Always stay aware of your surroundings while walking. Do not stare at the phone continuously while crossing roads.

title: Check Power Cut Schedule
steps: Step 1: Open your phone browser.
Step 2: Type your electricity board website (Example: TNEB power cut schedule).
Step 3: Enter your area name.
Step 4: Check date and timing of scheduled shutdown.
warning: Keep emergency light or torch ready if a long power cut is scheduled.

title: Verify UPI Payment Request Before Paying
steps: Step 1: Open your UPI app.
Step 2: Check if it says 'Pay' or 'Receive'.
Step 3: If it says 'Pay', money will go from your account.
Step 4: Check receiver name carefully.
Step 5: If you don't know the person, tap 'Decline'.
warning: You NEVER need to enter UPI PIN to receive money. That is a scam.
